issues.title: Mobile bottom nav layout overflow
issues.attack: 7 tabs with min-w-[64px] = 448px exceeds typical 360-390px mobile viewport, breaking owner mobile nav
issues.response: Valid. Plan must address mobile layout overflow with spacing/sizing adjustments.

issues.title: No test cases for role-gated nav rendering
issues.attack: Step 3 only runs existing tests. No tests verify owner sees org link or non-owner doesn't.
issues.response: Valid. Add basic tests for owner/non-owner nav rendering.

issues.title: Step 2 title contradicts body
issues.attack: Title says 'replaces reference' but code adds without removing reference.
issues.response: Misleading title. Design spec says add, not replace. Fix wording.

issues.title: --timeout=30 requires uninstalled pytest-timeout
issues.attack: pytest-timeout not in dependencies. Verification command will fail.

issues.title: Hardcoded URLs instead of {% url %}
issues.attack: Menu uses hardcoded /org/ instead of Django {% url %} tag.
issues.response: All existing nav links use hardcoded URLs. Introducing {% url %} for only org would be inconsistent. Codebase convention.

issues.title: DRY violation across nav files
issues.attack: Full org menu HTML duplicated in sidebar and mobile nav.
issues.response: Sidebar and mobile have different HTML structure/CSS. All existing items follow same pattern. Established convention.

issues.title: Position inconsistency between sidebar and mobile
issues.attack: Sidebar adds after settings, mobile adds before settings.
issues.response: Clarify to 'before settings' consistently in both.
